member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService
member.summary:
Manages deterministic environment isolation for every Oracle tool WEDM launches.
Guarantees: • Every OUI / WLST / OPatch / Forms / OHS invocation executes inside an explicitly controlled, sanitized environment — never inheriting ambient machine state. • Stale ORACLE_HOME, WL_HOME, MW_HOME, CLASSPATH, WLST_HOME, OPatch residuals, and JVM override variables are cleared before each tool runs. • TEMP / TMP is scoped to the current deployment session, preventing cross-session contamination from OUI JAR extraction residue. • PATH is reconstructed from scratch: Windows system directories + JavaHome\bin + tool-specific additions. Stale Oracle entries are excluded. • All mutations are process-scoped (PowerShell $env: injection) — machine-level PATH and registry are never modified by this service. • Pre/post deployment snapshots enable drift detection after each phase.
The primary integration pattern is → use as the first block in every PowerShell script body that launches an Oracle tool.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.CaptureSnapshot(WEDM.Domain.Models.SnapshotKind,System.Guid)
member.summary:
Captures a point-in-time snapshot of all Oracle-relevant environment variables from the current process environment (process + machine scope on Windows).
Call at three lifecycle points:
Before any deployment step runs —
After all forward steps complete —
After rollback completes —
Never throws — any read error is silently recorded in the snapshot.
member.param: The lifecycle point at which this snapshot is taken.
Session ID to embed in the snapshot for correlation.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.BuildContext(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration,WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentSnapshot)
member.summary:
Builds a for the given deployment session.
The context is the authoritative source of truth for all environment variables injected into Oracle tool subprocesses. It is constructed once at session start and mutated only to record path discoveries (e.g. after the OUI step resolves ).
Steps performed:
Resolve JavaHome, MiddlewareHome, OracleHome from config paths.
Create session-scoped TEMP root under config.Paths.TempDirectory.
Build sanitized PATH (system dirs + JavaHome\bin).
Apply isolation flags from the deployment config.
member.param: The deployment configuration for this session.
The snapshot captured before any deployment action ran. Used to initialise the context's baseline knowledge.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.BuildIsolatedEnvironment(WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleTool,WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext)
member.summary:
Produces a fully isolated, ready-to-inject environment variable set for the specified Oracle tool.
The returned is the primary output: prepend it verbatim to any PowerShell script body before the tool command. It contains:
Remove-Item Env:VAR -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue for every variable in
$env:VAR = 'value' for every variable in
Tool-specific variable sets:
— sets JAVA_HOME, TEMP, TMP, PATH; clears MW/WL/ORACLE vars
— sets ORACLE_HOME, JAVA_HOME, TEMP, PATH; clears WLST residuals
— sets ORACLE_HOME, JAVA_HOME, PATH (with OPatch dir); clears OPATCH_DEBUG
— sets JAVA_HOME, WL_HOME, TEMP; clears CLASSPATH
— sets ORACLE_HOME, JAVA_HOME, PATH; clears Forms residuals
— sets ORACLE_HOME, JAVA_HOME, PATH; clears OHS residuals
All tools: clear CLASSPATH, stale Oracle vars, JVM override vars
member.param: Which Oracle tool is being launched.
The session environment context.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.ValidateBeforeLaunch(WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleTool,WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables,WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext)
member.summary:
Validates that the environment is safe and correct for launching the specified Oracle tool. Call this immediately before handing off to the tool.
Checks performed:
JavaHome is set, non-empty, and java.exe exists at the expected path.
Tool-specific home (MiddlewareHome / OracleHome) is set and is a directory.
PATH does not contain stale Oracle or JDK entries that could shadow the intended runtime.
TEMP/TMP is the session-scoped directory (not %USERPROFILE% or system temp).
CLASSPATH is absent (to prevent classpath-based class loading surprises).
Tool-specific invariants (e.g. for OPatch: OPatch directory exists under OracleHome).
Returns = false when any blocker is found. Warnings are non-fatal; blockers prevent the tool from being launched.
member.param: The tool about to be launched.
The isolated environment variables that will be injected.
The session environment context.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.DetectDrift(WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentSnapshot,WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentSnapshot,WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext)
member.summary:
Compares to and returns a structured report of all detected environment mutations.
Mutations are classified as expected (e.g. WEDM itself set JAVA_HOME) or unexpected (e.g. an Oracle installer modified PATH or left TNS_ADMIN behind).
The list of expected variable names is derived from : any variable that appears in the session's or is treated as expected.
Oracle-installer-introduced additions to PATH are always flagged as unexpected drift regardless of whether they match expected patterns.
member.param: The earlier snapshot (typically ).
The later snapshot (typically ).
Used to classify mutations as expected or unexpected.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.ApplyScopedVariables(WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables)
member.summary:
Applies the variables in to the current process environment for the duration of the deployment step.
Scope rules:
Process-scoped (preferred) — written to Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable(name, value, Process). Automatically cleaned up when the engine process exits.
Session-scoped (rare) — written via PowerShell preamble injection only. Never persisted to machine or user scope.
The returned restores all variables to their pre-call values when disposed. Always wrap usage in a using block.
IMPORTANT: This method only applies variables to the current process. It does NOT affect subprocesses launched from PowerShell — those require the injection pattern.
member.param: The isolated environment variables to apply.
member.returns: A disposable scope that reverts all applied variables on disposal.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.GenerateDiagnostics(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext,WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentSnapshot)
member.summary:
Produces a structured diagnostics report for the completed deployment session.
The report includes:
Count of Oracle tool invocations that had isolation preamble applied.
Full list of variables cleared and injected during the session.
Environment drift report (pre vs post-deployment snapshot comparison).
Any validation warnings raised before tool launches.
PATH analysis: segment count, Oracle/Java segments, duplicates, stale entries.
Call after the last deployment step completes (or after rollback) to capture the full session diagnostic picture.
member.param: The session environment context.
Snapshot taken after all steps finished (or after rollback). When null, drift detection is skipped.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IEnvironmentIsolationService.AnalysePath(System.String,WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext)
member.summary:
Analyses a raw PATH string and classifies each segment.
Returns segment-level detail including: Oracle segments, Java segments, system segments, duplicates, stale entries, and any required entries that are missing from the PATH.
Used for diagnostics and for validating the sanitized PATH before injection.
member.param: The PATH string to analyse (semicolon-separated on Windows).
Session context; used to determine which paths are "expected".

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService
member.summary:
Manages the Oracle Central Inventory lifecycle for a deployment session.
Responsibilities: • Read and parse ContentsXML/inventory.xml • Detect registered Oracle homes (including stale/orphaned entries) • Detect active inventory lock files • Backup inventory.xml before any mutation • Surgically remove home entries without corrupting the XML • Validate pre-install and post-install inventory state • Log all inventory operations with structured diagnostics
This service is the single authority for all Oracle Central Inventory interactions. No step executor or rollback executor should read/write inventory.xml directly.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.ReadSnapshot(System.String)
member.summary: Parses the Oracle Central Inventory XML at the given inventory directory. Returns null when the inventory directory or inventory.xml does not exist. Never throws — parse errors are captured in .

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.ResolveInventoryXmlPath(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves the inventory.xml path inside the given inventory directory. Returns null when inventory.xml cannot be found.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.IsHomeRegistered(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when the given path appears in the Central Inventory at . Path comparison is case-insensitive and normalised (trailing slashes removed).

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.DetectHomeState(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Determines the current state of with respect to both the filesystem and the Central Inventory.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.FindOrphanedHomes(System.String)
member.summary: Returns all homes that are registered in the Central Inventory but whose target directories no longer exist (orphaned/stale registrations).

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.IsPartialInstall(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when the given directory exists but has an incomplete Oracle installation structure (i.e. partial/failed OUI). Checks for the presence of key marker directories: wlserver/, oracle_common/, inventory/.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.DetectLocks(System.String)
member.summary: Detects any active Oracle inventory lock files inside the inventory directory. Returns an empty list when the inventory is not locked.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.ValidateForInstall(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Validates the Oracle inventory state before launching OUI. Checks that: 1. The target home is NOT already registered 2. No inventory lock is active 3. No partial or orphaned install exists at the target path
Returns a with CanProceed=true when the inventory is clean and OUI may be safely launched.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.ValidateAfterInstall(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Validates the Oracle inventory state after a successful OUI exit. Checks that: 1. The target home IS now registered in the inventory 2. The inventory.xml is parseable 3. The target home directory structure is complete
Returns a with CanProceed=true when the post-install state is consistent.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.BackupInventoryXml(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a timestamped backup of inventory.xml before any mutation. Returns the backup file path, or null when the inventory.xml does not exist.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleInventoryService.RemoveHomeEntry(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Surgically removes all HOME and COMPOSITEHOME elements whose LOC attribute matches (case-insensitive) from inventory.xml.
Sequence: 1. Snapshot inventory state before removal 2. Create backup of inventory.xml 3. Load and mutate the XML (remove matching HOME elements) 4. Write updated XML back atomically 5. Snapshot inventory state after removal 6. Return full audit trail in
Never throws — errors are captured in .

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService
member.summary:
Governs the full lifecycle of Oracle middleware processes launched by WEDM.
Responsibilities: • Record ownership when WEDM launches an Oracle tool process. • Detect and classify all Oracle-related processes on the machine. • Build parent-child process trees via PPID relationships. • Execute staged shutdown (graceful → escalate → force-kill tree). • Detect orphan processes from prior WEDM sessions or crashed deployments. • Reconcile orphans: terminate WEDM-owned; report external ones for review. • Verify cleanup: confirm all target processes have exited. • Generate structured diagnostics for the deployment report.
Critical invariant: WEDM must never automatically terminate a process classified as . Such processes may belong to a running customer Oracle environment. They are flagged for operator review only.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.RegisterLaunch(WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessLaunchContext,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Records that WEDM has launched a process with the given PID. Must be called immediately after the process is started, before any await or delay that would allow the OS to reuse the PID.
The ownership record is persisted in the deployment session state so crash recovery can identify WEDM-owned orphans on next startup.
member.param: Launch metadata: session, deployment, tool, Oracle home, temp root.
The PID of the launched process.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.TrackChildProcess(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds a known child PID to an existing ownership record. Call when a launcher process spawns a child that WEDM tracks separately (e.g. OUI spawning a detached java subprocess).

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.DetectOracleProcesses
member.summary:
Scans all running processes and returns those classified as Oracle-related.
Classification is based on: • Process name (java, javaw, nodemanager, ohs, wlsvc, …) • Command line analysis (JVM args, classpath entries, -D properties) • Working directory (contains Oracle or WebLogic path markers) • Ownership records (is this PID registered with a WEDM session?)
Processes that cannot be classified with confidence are returned as and must not be killed automatically.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.GetOwnedProcesses(System.Guid)
member.summary: Returns all processes currently registered as owned by the specified WEDM session. Includes both root processes and tracked child processes. Returns an empty list when the session has no registered processes.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.BuildProcessTree(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Builds the complete parent-child process tree rooted at .
Uses WMI Win32_Process.ParentProcessId relationships to enumerate all descendants recursively. Returns null when the root process does not exist or has already exited.
The returned tree may include non-Oracle children (e.g. conhost.exe spawned by a PowerShell host). Each node carries its own so callers can filter before terminating.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.BuildSessionProcessTrees(System.Guid)
member.summary: Builds process trees for all currently registered Oracle processes in the session. Returns one tree per root process registered with .

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.ShutdownAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo},WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Executes staged shutdown for the specified Oracle processes:
Stage 1 — Graceful: send WM_CLOSE / CloseMainWindow. Stage 2 — Wait: pause for . Stage 3 — Escalate: send SIGTERM / taskkill /IM. Stage 4 — Force kill: Process.Kill(entireProcessTree: true).
External-owned processes are skipped with when is true.
Returns one per input process.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.CleanupSessionAsync(System.Guid,WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Stops all processes registered as owned by the specified session, then verifies they have exited.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.CleanupBeforeRetryAsync(System.Guid,System.Int32,WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Cleans up all Oracle processes from the previous attempt before the next retry begins.
Detects: • Orphan OUI Java processes from the prior attempt. • Stale WLST JVMs, OPatch JVMs, NodeManager remnants. • Temp directory locks from prior-attempt JARs.
Only terminates WEDM-owned processes from the prior attempt. External Oracle processes are reported but never terminated.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.DetectOrphans(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid})
member.summary:
Detects Oracle processes that appear to be orphaned from prior WEDM sessions or from crashed deployments.
Classification: • — launched by a known prior WEDM session. • — unclassified Oracle JVM; may be orphan. • — NOT a WEDM orphan; do not touch.

member.summary:
Scans for stale WEDM deployment sessions and associated orphan Oracle processes. Called at WEDM startup to identify and offer cleanup of crash remnants.
Returns a report describing: • Orphan processes found from prior deployment sessions. • Which processes are safe to terminate automatically. • Which processes require operator review before cleanup. • Stale temp directories left by prior OUI extractions.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.IOracleProcessLifecycleService.PrepareForRollbackAsync(System.String,System.Guid,WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Performs the process cleanup required before rollback can safely proceed: 1. Stop Oracle processes (NodeManager, WLST, OPatch, OUI remnants). 2. Stop Forms / OHS / AdminServer / ManagedServer. 3. Verify Oracle Home is no longer locked by any process.
Returns a report; the rollback executor inspects to determine whether it is safe to delete Oracle Home directories.

member.summary: Verifies that none of the specified PIDs are still running. Returns the list of processes that are still alive (should be empty on success).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService
member.summary:
Governs the vault-backed secret lifecycle for recoverable deployment sessions.
Purpose: eliminate the R-03 bug where checkpoint persistence redacted runtime secrets with "***REDACTED***", making resumed sessions unable to authenticate against Oracle tools, WLST, RCU, or SSL endpoints.
Contract: • PersistAndBind() — called on every checkpoint save; vaults each secret and replaces the config field with a vault-reference sentinel. • Rehydrate() — called on session resume; resolves every sentinel back to its plaintext value from the DPAPI vault. • ValidateForResume() — called before resume; returns a blocking result if any required secret cannot be resolved.
Invariants: • Plaintext values are NEVER written to checkpoint JSON. • Legacy "***REDACTED***" values produce diagnostic errors, never silent failures. • DPAPI scope mismatches (different user) produce actionable operator warnings. • All diagnostics output aliases and field names only — no secret values.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService.PersistAndBind(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration,System.Guid)
member.summary:
Persists each runtime secret from to the DPAPI vault under a deterministic alias, then replaces every secret field in with a vault-reference sentinel of the form "__WEDM_VAULT_REF:{alias}__".
Call this on the cloned configuration just before writing the checkpoint to disk. The resulting config object is safe to serialize — it contains no plaintext secrets.
Returns one binding per secret field processed.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService.Rehydrate(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration,System.Guid)
member.summary:
Resolves vault-reference sentinels in back to their plaintext values by decrypting the corresponding DPAPI vault entries.
Mutates in-place: sentinel field values are replaced with the decrypted plaintext.
Returns a result describing which secrets were resolved and which were not. Callers must check before proceeding.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService.ValidateForResume(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration,System.Guid)
member.summary:
Validates that all required secrets in can be resolved from the DPAPI vault without actually writing any values into the config.
Call before to produce operator-friendly diagnostics and block resume early when recovery is impossible.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService.HasLegacyPlaceholders(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration)
member.summary: Returns true when any secret field in contains a legacy "***REDACTED***" placeholder that cannot be recovered from the vault.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService.HasVaultReferences(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration)
member.summary: Returns true when any secret field in contains a vault-reference sentinel written by .

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Interfaces.ISecretRehydrationService.GetDiagnostics(WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration,System.Guid)
member.summary: Returns metadata-only diagnostics describing the current secret state of and the associated DPAPI vault. NEVER exposes plaintext values, encrypted blobs, or credential material.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration.CurrentInstallerContext
member.summary: Per-attempt installer execution context set by before each OUI step invocation. Null on first attempt when isolation is disabled. Not serialised — rebuilt fresh on each engine startup / retry.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration.OracleRollback
member.summary: Oracle rollback report accumulated by Oracle-aware rollback executors during a rollback pass. Each Oracle rollback executor (OracleInstallRollbackExecutor, OracleFormsReportsRollbackExecutor, etc.) writes its per-step results here. The workflow engine attaches this to after the full rollback pass completes. Null until the first Oracle rollback executor runs. Not serialised — runtime-only.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentConfiguration.EnvironmentContext
member.summary:
Per-session environment isolation context. Built once at session start by and stored here so that all step executors can access the sanitized JavaHome, PATH, TempRoot, and per-tool isolation preambles without re-reading machine environment state.
Null when the environment isolation subsystem is not initialised (e.g. in unit tests that construct DeploymentConfiguration directly without the full DI stack). Step executors that consume this must guard against null and fall back to ambient environment variables when absent.
Not serialised — runtime-only; rebuilt from config on each engine startup.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentLockDescriptor
member.summary:
Machine-wide deployment lock metadata for a guarded Oracle resource.
Lock file format (v2): A single {sanitizedKey}.meta file in the lock directory. The file IS the lock — its existence means the resource is held. Created via atomic provisional-then-rename protocol: 1. Write {sanitizedKey}-{sessionId:N}.meta.tmp (provisional, fully flushed) 2. File.Move(provisional, meta, overwrite: false) — atomic exclusive gate Released by deleting the .meta file.
Backward compatibility: v1 locks wrote both an empty .lock sentinel and a .meta data file. == 1 identifies these; reconciliation removes the orphaned .lock file during startup. The .meta content is otherwise identical.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentLockDescriptor.LockFileVersion
member.summary: Lock file format version. 1 = legacy (empty .lock + .meta pair). 2 = current (single .meta file, atomic provisional-rename protocol).

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentLockDescriptor.IsStale(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: True when the heartbeat is older than .

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentLockDescriptor.IsFromDifferentMachine
member.summary: True when the lock was acquired by a different machine.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.LogEntry
member.summary: Structured log entry for audit trail.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentSessionState
member.summary: Durable deployment session persisted for crash recovery, resume, and operator diagnostics. Written atomically after each workflow checkpoint.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentSessionState.SecretReferences
member.summary:
Vault-backed references for each secret field that was present at checkpoint time. Populated by and used by diagnostics and operator tooling.
These references contain ONLY aliases and metadata — no secret values. The actual encrypted blobs live in the DPAPI vault file, not in this session file.
Backward compatibility: old checkpoints without this field deserialize with an empty list, which the rehydration service handles by detecting legacy placeholders and producing actionable remediation guidance.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentStepSnapshot
member.summary: JSON-serializable step snapshot for checkpoint restore. Includes so that rollback after a process crash uses the original captured Oracle paths rather than config-derived fallbacks. Old checkpoint files that pre-date compensation serialisation will have == null, which the rollback executors handle gracefully by falling back to paths.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentStepSnapshot.RollbackCompensation
member.summary: Persisted Oracle rollback compensation data captured when the step succeeded. Null for non-Oracle steps or when the step has not yet succeeded. When present and this snapshot is restored via , the compensation's is changed from to so diagnostics can distinguish crash-recovery from live execution. Old checkpoint files without this field deserialise with null — the rollback executors fall back to config-derived paths in that case ().

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentStep
member.summary: Represents a single atomic step within a deployment workflow. Steps are orchestrated by the DeploymentWorkflowEngine and executed in order. Each step is independently retryable, loggable, and rollback-aware.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentStep.RollbackCompensation
member.summary: Oracle-aware compensation record populated immediately after this step succeeds. Consumed by Oracle rollback executors to precisely reverse the Oracle state created here. Serialised into at each workflow checkpoint so that rollback after a process crash uses the original captured paths, not config-derived fallbacks. Null when the step has not yet succeeded, the step is not Oracle-specific, or when the step did not produce Oracle state changes requiring compensation. When restored from a checkpoint, is ; when captured live it is .

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.StepExecutionResult
member.summary: Represents the result of executing a step's action — returned by automation executors.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.StepExecutionResult.ManualInterventionRequired
member.summary: Set for rollback helpers that succeeded in recording state but cannot fully reverse (e.g. schema drop requires DBA RCU/sqlplus). Rollback summaries surface this distinctly from hard failures.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentSnapshot
member.summary:
Point-in-time snapshot of the machine environment variables relevant to Oracle middleware.
Captured at three lifecycle points: • — before any WEDM action runs • — after all steps complete • — after rollback completes
Snapshots are compared by EnvironmentDriftDetector to identify unexpected mutations introduced by Oracle tooling or external processes.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentSnapshot.OraclePathSegments
member.summary: Returns true when a PATH segment contains a known Oracle/JVM middleware pattern. Used for contamination detection.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext
member.summary:
Per-deployment-session runtime environment context. Built once at session start and stored on .
This is the authoritative source of truth for all environment variables injected into Oracle tool subprocesses. Every tool that WEDM launches reads its environment from here rather than from the ambient machine state.
Not serialised — rebuilt from config on each engine startup.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.MiddlewareHome
member.summary: Middleware home (WLS_HOME parent). Set after install; empty before.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.OracleHome
member.summary: Oracle Home — same as MiddlewareHome for WLS 12c+; may differ for 11g.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.OpatchHome
member.summary: OPatch directory under OracleHome.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.InventoryLocation
member.summary: Oracle Central Inventory path.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.JavaHome

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.JavaExe
member.summary: Resolved java.exe path (JavaHome\bin\java.exe).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.TempRoot
member.summary: Session-scoped temp root. All WEDM-spawned processes use this as TEMP/TMP. Prevents cross-session contamination from OUI JAR extraction residue. Format: <config.Paths.TempDirectory>\wedm-session-<SessionId[..8]>\

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.WorkingDirectory
member.summary: Working directory for OUI invocations (same as TempRoot).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.SanitizedPath
member.summary: The sanitized PATH string for this session. Contains only: JavaHome\bin + Windows system dirs. Oracle tool-specific paths are added per-tool by .

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.PreDeploymentSnapshot
member.summary: Machine environment captured before any deployment action ran.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.ClearClasspath
member.summary: When true, CLASSPATH is explicitly cleared for all Oracle tool invocations.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.ClearStaleOracleVars
member.summary: When true, stale ORACLE_HOME / WL_HOME / MW_HOME are explicitly unset.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.ClearWlstResiduals
member.summary: When true, WLST_HOME / WLST_PROPERTIES are cleared before WLST runs.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.ClearJvmOverrideVars
member.summary: When true, JAVA_OPTS / _JAVA_OPTIONS / JVM_ARGS are cleared to prevent heap override.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.DeploymentEnvironmentContext.ClearOpatchResiduals
member.summary: When true, OPATCH_DEBUG / related OPatch residuals are cleared.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables
member.summary:
Computed set of environment variables for a specific Oracle tool invocation. Produced by .
The string is the key deliverable — it contains all $env:VAR = 'value' and Remove-Item Env:VAR statements that must be prepended to every PowerShell body that launches an Oracle tool.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables.Tool
member.summary: The Oracle tool these variables are scoped to.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables.SetVariables
member.summary: Variables to SET in the child process environment. Key = variable name, Value = value.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables.ClearVariables
member.summary: Variable names to explicitly REMOVE (unset) in the child process environment. These are variables that must not be present to avoid contaminating the tool.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables.PowerShellPreamble
member.summary: Ready-to-inject PowerShell block: Remove-Item Env: + $env:VAR = statements. Prepend this to any PowerShell script body before launching an Oracle tool.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables.DiagnosticSummary
member.summary: Summary description for logging / diagnostics.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.IsolatedEnvironmentVariables.BuiltAt
member.summary: When built in this session.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentIsolationReport
member.summary: Report produced at the end of a deployment session summarising all environment isolation activity: what was cleared, what was injected, any drift detected.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentIsolationReport.PreambleInjectionCount
member.summary: Number of Oracle tool invocations that had the isolation preamble applied.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentIsolationReport.IsolatedToolInvocations
member.summary: Tools that were launched with the isolation preamble.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentDriftReport
member.summary:
Compares two instances and reports unexpected mutations.
Populated by EnvironmentDriftDetector.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentDriftReport.HasDrift
member.summary: True when any unexpected mutation was detected.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentDriftReport.Findings
member.summary: All detected mutations (both expected and unexpected).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentDriftReport.UnexpectedFindings
member.summary: Only the unexpected mutations — the operator should review these.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentDriftReport.Summary
member.summary: Summary string for log output.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentDriftFinding.IsExpected
member.summary: True when this mutation was expected (e.g. WEDM itself set JAVA_HOME post-install). False when it was introduced by an external process or Oracle installer residue.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.EnvironmentValidationResult
member.summary: Result of pre-launch environment validation for a given Oracle tool.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.PathAnalysisResult
member.summary: Result of PATH analysis: what's clean, what's stale, what's missing.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass
member.summary: Classifies the root cause of an OUI installer failure so that the retry engine can apply the correct remediation before the next attempt.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.Unknown
member.summary: Failure cause is undetermined (default).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.InventoryConflict
member.summary: Oracle Central Inventory already has a registration for the target home (INST-07319 / "Oracle Home already exists"). Remediation: remove the stale inventory entry and delete the partial directory.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.LockConflict
member.summary: Active inventory lock file prevents OUI from starting. Remediation: wait for the lock owner to finish or remove a stale lock file.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.Timeout
member.summary: Installer did not complete within the configured timeout window. Remediation: increase OuiInstallTimeoutMinutes or investigate hanging OUI process.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.PrerequisiteFailure
member.summary: Prerequisite check failed (insufficient disk space, missing VC runtime, etc.). Remediation: satisfy the blocking prerequisite before retrying.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.ResponseFileIssue
member.summary: OUI could not parse or locate the response file / silent XML. Remediation: regenerate the response file in the isolated temp directory.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.JavaLaunchFailure
member.summary: java.exe not found or JVM crashed at startup. Remediation: verify JDK is installed and JAVA_HOME is set correctly.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.ExtractionFailure
member.summary: OUI failed to extract the installer JAR (temp directory full or locked). Remediation: purge the extraction directory and retry with a clean temp path.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerFailureClass.PartialInstall
member.summary: OUI wrote partial artifacts to the middleware home but did not complete. Remediation: remove partial directory and inventory entry, then retry.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext
member.summary:
Immutable context for a single OUI installer execution attempt.
Every retry gets a fresh with: • Unique temp, extraction, and log directories (prevents state bleed across retries) • Per-attempt response file and inventory pointer paths • Previous failure classification (informs pre-flight remediation choices) • Cleanup manifest (paths to delete after attempt, success or failure)
The context is constructed by and stored on before each OUI step is invoked. OUI steps read it to configure their execution paths; they never create directories or response files from config paths directly when a context is present.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.AttemptNumber
member.summary: 1-based attempt number (1 = first attempt, 2 = first retry, …).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.UniqueId
member.summary: Unique run identifier used to build all per-attempt path names.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.TempDirectory
member.summary: Isolated temporary directory for this attempt. Response files, inventory pointer, and scratch files are written here.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.ExtractionDirectory
member.summary: Isolated JAR extraction directory. Passed to OUI as -Djava.io.tmpdir to prevent extraction artefacts from contaminating the system or shared temp directory.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.ResponseFilePath
member.summary: Absolute path of the generated .rsp response file for this attempt.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.SilentXmlPath
member.summary: Absolute path of the WLS 11g silent XML file (null for 12c/14c).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.InventoryPointerPath
member.summary: Absolute path of the oraInst.loc inventory pointer written for this attempt.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.OuiLogDirectory
member.summary: Directory where OUI log candidates are expected for this attempt. Used to scope the log scanner's search.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.PreviousFailureClass
member.summary: Failure classification from the PREVIOUS attempt (Unknown on first attempt). Used by the pre-flight validator to apply targeted remediation.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerExecutionContext.CleanupPaths
member.summary: Paths created by this context that should be purged after the attempt completes (regardless of success/failure) to avoid accumulation of stale extraction artefacts.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerRetryPreflightResult
member.summary: Result of the retry pre-flight validation performed before each OUI attempt.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerRetryPreflightResult.CanProceed
member.summary: True when all pre-flight checks passed and OUI may safely launch.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerRetryPreflightResult.Findings
member.summary: Human-readable summary of each check performed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerRetryPreflightResult.BlockingItems
member.summary: Items that prevent launch (CanProceed will be false when this is non-empty).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.InstallerRetryPreflightResult.ActionsTaken
member.summary: Actions taken during preflight (cleanup performed, etc.).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.JdkInstallationDiagnostics
member.summary: Captured diagnostics from a JDK install attempt (reporting + troubleshooting).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.LocalPayloadFolderDefinition
member.summary: Defines one subfolder under D:\WEDM\{version}\ and how to match installers inside it.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.LocalPayloadFolderDefinition.DirectoryPayload
member.summary: When true, validation checks directory structure instead of a single file (e.g. webutil).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.MigrationConfiguration
member.summary: Dedicated session model for upgrade / migrate workflows. Kept separate from to avoid mixing deployment and migration concerns.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.MigrationExecutionCredentials
member.summary: In-memory credentials — never persisted to disk.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.MigrationSessionDocument
member.summary: Versioned migration session envelope for export/import and resume workflows.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState
member.summary: Describes the observed state of a target Oracle Home path with respect to the Oracle Central Inventory and the local filesystem.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.Clean
member.summary: Not registered in inventory and folder does not exist — safe to install.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.RegisteredAndPresent
member.summary: Registered in inventory AND directory structure is complete — already installed.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.RegisteredOrphaned
member.summary: Registered in inventory but target folder is absent — stale orphaned entry.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.PartialInstall
member.summary: Target folder exists and appears partially populated (missing key subdirectories such as wlserver/, oracle_common/, or modules/), but is NOT in the inventory. Indicates a previous failed or interrupted OUI session.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.UnregisteredInstall
member.summary: Target folder exists and contains enough structure to suggest a full install, but is NOT registered in the Central Inventory — unusual state.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.InventoryLocked
member.summary: A lock file exists inside the inventory directory — active OUI/OPatch operation.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleHomeState.Unknown
member.summary: State cannot be determined (e.g., inventory.xml is corrupt or unreadable).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult
member.summary: Result of an Oracle inventory pre-install or post-install validation check.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.CanProceed
member.summary: True when the inventory state allows OUI installation to proceed safely.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.HomeState
member.summary: Observed state of the target Oracle Home path.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.TargetMiddlewareHome
member.summary: Absolute path of the target middleware home being validated.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.OracleInventoryPath
member.summary: Absolute path of the Oracle Central Inventory directory.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.Snapshot
member.summary: Inventory snapshot captured during validation (null if inventory unreadable).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.Findings
member.summary: Human-readable summary of each check performed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.RemediationSteps
member.summary: Operator-actionable remediation steps when is false.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.IsLocked
member.summary: True when an inventory lock file was detected.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.OrphanedHomes
member.summary: Homes registered in the inventory that reference directories which no longer exist (stale/orphaned registrations that should be cleaned up).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryValidationResult.ConflictingHomes
member.summary: Homes that appear in the inventory at the exact target path or as conflicting entries.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult
member.summary: Result of an Oracle inventory home entry removal operation. Captures before/after snapshots to support audit and rollback verification.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.Success
member.summary: True when the removal completed without error.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.Message
member.summary: Human-readable summary of the operation.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.BackupPath
member.summary: Path to the inventory.xml backup created before mutation (null if not created).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.InventoryXmlPath
member.summary: Path to the inventory.xml that was mutated.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.SnapshotBefore
member.summary: Inventory snapshot captured immediately before the removal.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.SnapshotAfter
member.summary: Inventory snapshot captured immediately after the removal.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.HomeWasRegistered
member.summary: True when the target home was actually found and removed from inventory.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.RemovedEntries
member.summary: XML element names/LOC values of the removed entries.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryRemovalResult.Error
member.summary: Error message when is false.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleInventoryLockDescriptor
member.summary: Describes an active Oracle inventory lock file.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.CompensationSource
member.summary: Indicates how the record was obtained for a given rollback operation. Used for diagnostics, audit logging, and the rollback report to distinguish live-captured from checkpoint-restored state.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.CompensationSource.None
member.summary: No compensation record was available for this step (non-Oracle step).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.CompensationSource.Runtime
member.summary: Compensation was captured live during the current execution session, immediately after the corresponding install step succeeded. This is the normal case for a fresh deployment that hasn't crashed.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.CompensationSource.Restored
member.summary: Compensation was restored from a persisted deployment session checkpoint. Indicates the deployment session was resumed after a process crash or restart. Rollback paths are the original install-time paths, not the current config paths.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.CompensationSource.Fallback
member.summary: No compensation record was available — rollback is using config-derived paths as a fallback. This occurs when rolling back a session whose checkpoint predates compensation serialisation, or when the step's compensation was never captured (e.g. install step never reached success). Paths may differ from what was actually installed if retry isolation changed TempDirectory.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation
member.summary:
Per-step compensation record populated by each Oracle installer step immediately after it succeeds. Stored on so that Oracle rollback executors can precisely reverse the state this specific step created, rather than relying solely on paths (which may have changed between steps).
Lifecycle: • Created by OUI-launching steps (InstallWebLogic, InstallFormsReports, InstallOHSWebTier, ConfigureJavaHome) before returning . • Serialised into at each workflow checkpoint so that rollback after a process crash uses the original captured paths rather than config-derived fallbacks. • Read by the corresponding Oracle rollback executor during rollback. • Null when the step has not yet succeeded or the step is not Oracle-specific.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.Source
member.summary: Indicates how this compensation record was obtained. Set to when captured live. Changed to by when deserialized from a checkpoint.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.OracleHomePaths
member.summary: Oracle Home paths created by this step. For InstallWebLogic: [config.Paths.MiddlewareHome]. For InstallOHSWebTier: [OHS_Home path]. For InstallFormsReports: [Forms/Reports home path].

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.OracleInventoryPath
member.summary: Oracle Central Inventory path used during this install.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.InventorySnapshotBefore
member.summary: Oracle Central Inventory state captured immediately before OUI launched. Enables before/after diffs in the rollback report and confirms which homes were registered by this specific step.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.CreatedServiceNames
member.summary: Names of Windows services registered by or for this step (as they appear in SCM). For example: "WLS_AdminServer", "WLS NodeManager", "OracleOHSComponent1". Rollback executors stop and remove these before deleting the home directory.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.SetEnvironmentVariableNames
member.summary: Machine-level environment variable names set by this step. For ConfigureJavaHome: ["JAVA_HOME"]. For ConfigureRegistry: ["ORACLE_HOME"]. Rollback executors remove these (matching the configured value to avoid false removals).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.CreatedRegistryKeyPaths
member.summary: Full HKLM paths of registry keys created by this step. For example: @"SOFTWARE\ORACLE\KEY_WEDM_OracleMW".

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.GeneratedFilePaths
member.summary: Response files, silent XML files, inventory pointer files, and other WEDM-generated artefacts written by this step. Cleaned up during rollback after the home directory is removed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.AppliedPatchIds
member.summary: Oracle patch IDs (PSU/RU) applied by this step. When non-empty, rollback guidance includes OPatch-rollback steps.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackCompensation.CapturedAt
member.summary: UTC timestamp when this record was captured (immediately after step success).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackVerificationResult
member.summary: Outcome of Oracle environment verification run after rollback completes. Produced by (engine) and attached to the deployment report for operator review.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackVerificationResult.IsClean
member.summary: True when all targeted Oracle state has been confirmed reversed: • Target home is no longer registered in the Central Inventory • No Oracle inventory lock files remain • No orphan OUI / java-OUI processes detected • Target home directory does not exist

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackVerificationResult.Findings
member.summary: All verification findings — both pass (✔) and fail (✗). Always populated for audit completeness even when IsClean = true.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackVerificationResult.RemainingWarnings
member.summary: Non-fatal observations that do not prevent future installs but the operator should be aware of (e.g. stale temp files, partial lock files).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackVerificationResult.ManualActionsRequired
member.summary: Actions the operator must complete manually before the environment is considered fully clean and ready for a fresh install attempt.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport
member.summary:
Oracle-specific rollback narrative surfaced in the deployment report. Aggregated from all Oracle rollback executor runs during a single rollback pass. Attached to for HTML report rendering.
Accumulation pattern: Each Oracle rollback executor writes to (initialising it on first write). The workflow engine copies it into the report after rollback.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.DryRunMode
member.summary: When true, rollback was run in dry-run mode. All operations were logged but no destructive actions (process termination, filesystem deletion, inventory mutation) were performed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.RemovedHomes
member.summary: Oracle Home directories that were deleted during rollback.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.DetachedInventoryEntries
member.summary: Central Inventory entries that were detached (format: "NAME @ PATH").

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.StoppedAndRemovedServices
member.summary: Windows services that were stopped and removed from SCM.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.RemovedEnvironmentVariables
member.summary: Machine-level environment variables that were removed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.StoppedProcesses
member.summary: Oracle / JVM processes that were stopped (format: "PID processName").

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.RemovedGeneratedFiles
member.summary: WEDM-generated files (response files, silent XML, etc.) that were deleted.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.InventoryClean
member.summary: True when the Central Inventory is confirmed clean post-rollback.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.NoOuiLocks
member.summary: True when no Oracle inventory lock files remain in the inventory directory.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.NoOrphanProcesses
member.summary: True when no orphan Oracle/JVM middleware processes remain running.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.RemainingWarnings
member.summary: Non-blocking warnings (files locked by the OS, stale temp directories the executor couldn't delete, etc.).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.ManualActionsRequired
member.summary: Actions the operator must complete manually before the environment is ready for a fresh install attempt. Examples: RCU schema drop, OPatch rollback, VC++ uninstall.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.GeneratedAt
member.summary: When this report section was generated.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.IsFullyClean
member.summary: True when the environment is considered fully clean for a retry.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport.MergeFrom(WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleRollbackReport)
member.summary: Merges another report's lists into this one (used when multiple executors write to the same report).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchConfiguration
member.summary: OPatch / PSU automation configuration.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchConfiguration.Enabled
member.summary: When true, OPatch workflow steps are included in the deployment plan.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchConfiguration.StandalonePatchWorkflow
member.summary: When true, the workflow engine returns a patch-only step plan (middleware home must already exist with OPatch).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchConfiguration.PatchStagingDirectory
member.summary: Directory containing patch folders (each with patch.xml) or a single patch directory.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchConfiguration.UseNapply
member.summary: Use opatch napply for the staging directory; when false, applies each immediate child patch sequentially.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchConfiguration.OpatchBatPathOverride
member.summary: Optional explicit path to opatch.bat; when empty, resolved under ORACLE_HOME.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchReport
member.summary: Structured output for patch compliance reporting.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.PatchReadinessResult
member.summary: Result of patch-environment checks plus optional live opatch version output.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.PayloadAcquisitionConfiguration
member.summary: Controls prerequisite installer detection, download, and caching.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.PayloadAcquisitionConfiguration.UseLocalRepositoryOnly
member.summary: When true, WEDM uses only the local repository under (no downloads).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind
member.summary: Classification of an Oracle-related process by its functional role. Used to determine the correct shutdown sequence and to protect customer-owned processes.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.OUI
member.summary: Oracle Universal Installer java subprocess (-jar fmw_*.jar -silent).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.WLST
member.summary: WebLogic Scripting Tool JVM (wlst.cmd / wlst.sh).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.OPatch
member.summary: Oracle OPatch JVM (opatch.bat / opatch napply).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.NodeManager
member.summary: WebLogic NodeManager daemon process.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.AdminServer
member.summary: WebLogic Administration Server (weblogic.Server -name AdminServer).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.ManagedServer
member.summary: WebLogic Managed Server (weblogic.Server -name [managed-name]).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.FormsRuntime
member.summary: Oracle Forms runtime JVM or installer.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.ReportsRuntime
member.summary: Oracle Reports runtime process.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.OHS
member.summary: Oracle HTTP Server (httpd / ohs) process.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.JdkInstaller
member.summary: JDK installer (java.exe / msiexec) launched by WEDM.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.RCU
member.summary: Repository Creation Utility (rcu.bat) JVM.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.OrphanJvm
member.summary: Unclassified Java/JVM process with Oracle middleware markers in the command line. May be an orphaned subprocess; treat cautiously.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessKind.Unknown
member.summary: Cannot be classified with confidence. MUST NOT be terminated automatically — requires operator review.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessOwnership
member.summary: Process ownership classification: who launched this process?

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessOwnership.WedmOwned
member.summary: Launched by this WEDM session — safe to manage.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessOwnership.WedmPriorSession
member.summary: Appears to have been launched by a prior WEDM session — orphan candidate.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessOwnership.External
member.summary: Launched by an external actor (customer deployment, another product). WEDM must NOT terminate external processes automatically.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessOwnership.Unknown
member.summary: Ownership cannot be determined from available evidence.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage
member.summary: The stage at which a process was terminated during staged shutdown.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage.AlreadyExited
member.summary: Process had already exited before termination was attempted.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage.Graceful
member.summary: Process stopped cleanly in response to CloseMainWindow / graceful signal.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage.Escalated
member.summary: Process stopped after the graceful timeout expired (escalated kill).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage.ForcedKill
member.summary: Process was force-killed including its entire process tree.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage.Failed
member.summary: Termination failed — process is still running.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.TerminationStage.Skipped
member.summary: Process was skipped because it is externally owned.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo
member.summary: Rich information about a single detected Oracle-related process. Extends the basic with ownership, tree, and classification data.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.DetectedJvmArgs
member.summary: Key JVM arguments detected (e.g. -Dweblogic.Name, -Djava.class.path fragment).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.OracleHomeRefs
member.summary: Oracle middleware home references found in command line or JVM args.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.OwnerSessionId
member.summary: Session ID of the WEDM session that owns this process, if known.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.OwnerAttemptNumber
member.summary: Deployment attempt number of the WEDM session that owns this process, if known.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.Runtime
member.summary: How long this process has been running as of detection time.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.HoldsInventoryLock
member.summary: True when this process holds a file lock on a known Oracle inventory path.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessInfo.LockedPaths
member.summary: Paths this process appears to have locked (file handles in Oracle directories).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessTree
member.summary: Parent-child process tree rooted at a single Oracle process. Built by ProcessTreeBuilder via WMI PPID relationships.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessTree.Root
member.summary: The root process of this tree.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessTree.Children
member.summary: Direct children of the root.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessTree.All
member.summary: Flat enumeration of all processes in this tree (root + all descendants).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessTree.TotalCount
member.summary: Total number of processes in this tree.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OracleProcessTree.HasInventoryLock
member.summary: True when any process in the tree holds an Oracle inventory lock.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessOwnershipRecord
member.summary: Records metadata about a process launched by WEDM. Persisted in the deployment session state so that crash recovery can identify WEDM-owned orphan processes on next startup.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessLaunchContext
member.summary: Context provided to the lifecycle service when WEDM is about to launch an Oracle tool. Used to record the launch and associate subsequent child processes with this session.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessTerminationResult
member.summary: Result of terminating a single process during staged shutdown.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy
member.summary: Shutdown policy for a process lifecycle operation. Controls timeout durations and force-kill behaviour.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy.GracefulTimeout
member.summary: Time to wait after sending WM_CLOSE / CloseMainWindow before escalating.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy.EscalationTimeout
member.summary: Time to wait after escalation before force-killing the process tree.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy.ForceKillOnEscalationFailure
member.summary: When true, force-kill the entire process tree if escalation also fails.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy.SkipExternalProcesses
member.summary: When true, skip external-owned processes instead of returning an error.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ShutdownPolicy.DryRun
member.summary: When true, log all actions but perform no actual process operations (dry-run).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.OrphanReconciliationOptions
member.summary: Options for orphan reconciliation (safe cleanup of prior-session Oracle processes).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OrphanReconciliationOptions.PriorSessionIds
member.summary: Session IDs to consider as "prior WEDM sessions" for orphan detection.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OrphanReconciliationOptions.IncludeUnknownJvms
member.summary: When true, also consider any unknown Oracle JVM as an orphan candidate.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OrphanReconciliationOptions.AutoTerminateWedmOrphans
member.summary: When true, terminate WEDM-owned orphans automatically.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.OrphanReconciliationOptions.SuggestExternalCleanup
member.summary: When true, log suggestions for external orphans but do NOT terminate them. External Oracle processes are never killed automatically regardless of this setting.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessLifecycleReport
member.summary: Structured report produced by a process lifecycle operation. Covers detection, termination, orphan reconciliation, and verification.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessClassificationResult
member.summary: Classification signal used internally by OracleProcessClassifier. Tracks how confident the classifier is and which evidence led to the classification.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessClassificationResult.ExtractedOracleHomes
member.summary: Oracle middleware home paths extracted from the command line.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProcessClassificationResult.ExtractedJvmArgs
member.summary: Relevant JVM arguments extracted from the command line.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.WedmReleaseChannelKind
member.summary: Logical release train for future update feeds (no network transport in core).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.WedmProductSidecar
member.summary: Optional sidecar metadata shipped next to WEDM.exe (Resources/wedm-product.json).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.WedmUpdateManifest
member.summary: Portable update manifest (local file today; future HTTP feed consumer).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.WedmReleaseBundleManifest
member.summary: Enterprise release bundle descriptor (checksums produced by packaging scripts).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProductVersionSnapshot
member.summary: Resolved product identity for UI and reports (no secrets).

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.ProductVersionSnapshot.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolved product identity for UI and reports (no secrets).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary
member.summary: Structured record of a rollback session produced by DeploymentWorkflowEngine.RollbackAsync. Replaces the previous bare return value, providing operator-grade transparency about exactly which steps were reversed, which had no registered executor, and which failed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.FullyRolledBack
member.summary: True only when every eligible step was reversed by its executor with no failures and no missing executors. A partial rollback (some steps had no executor or failed) leaves this false.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.StepsRolledBack
member.summary: Count of steps that were successfully reversed by their rollback executors.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.StepsNoExecutor
member.summary: Count of steps eligible for rollback but with no executor registered for their RollbackAction. These steps remain on disk and require manual operator intervention.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.StepsFailed
member.summary: Count of steps where the rollback executor returned failure or threw an exception.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.StepsManualInterventionRequired
member.summary: Executor reported success but the operation still requires explicit operator action (RCU drops, VC++ uninstall, third-party installers). Not counted as automated reversal.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.Records
member.summary: Ordered records, one per eligible step (reverse execution order).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackSummary.OracleDetails
member.summary: Oracle-specific rollback narrative accumulated by Oracle rollback executors during the rollback pass. Populated by DeploymentWorkflowEngine after the full rollback pass completes by copying DeploymentConfiguration.OracleRollback. Null when no Oracle rollback executors ran (e.g. rollback of non-Oracle steps only).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackStepRecord
member.summary: Individual rollback outcome for one deployment step.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackStepRecord.Sequence
member.summary: Original execution sequence number of the step being reversed.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackStepRecord.Outcome
member.summary: One of: RolledBack, ManualInterventionRequired, NoExecutor, Failed, Exception.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.RollbackStepRecord.CompensationSource
member.summary: How the rollback compensation data was obtained for this step. — captured live; most precise. — recovered from crash-recovery checkpoint. — no compensation available; config paths used. — step had no Oracle state to reverse.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind
member.summary: Discriminates the category of secret stored in a vault binding. Used for diagnostics and structured auditing — never contains secret values.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.AdminPassword
member.summary: WebLogic Administration Server password.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.SysPassword
member.summary: Oracle database SYS account password (RCU prerequisite).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.SchemaPassword
member.summary: RCU schema owner password.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.IdentityKeystorePassword
member.summary: PKCS#12 identity keystore password.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.TrustKeystorePassword
member.summary: Trust keystore password.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.NodeManagerPassword
member.summary: NodeManager password.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.JdbcPassword
member.summary: JDBC datasource password.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretKind.Generic
member.summary: Any other credential not covered by a specific kind.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretResolutionSource
member.summary: Indicates where a secret value came from during rehydration.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretResolutionSource.DpapiVault
member.summary: Secret was decrypted from the DPAPI vault on this machine/user.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretResolutionSource.ConfigPlaintext
member.summary: Secret value is available as runtime plaintext in configuration (no vault reference present — not yet checkpointed).

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretResolutionSource.NotResolved
member.summary: Vault entry was not found — secret cannot be resolved.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretResolutionSource.LegacyPlaceholder
member.summary: Checkpoint contains a legacy "***REDACTED***" placeholder written by older WEDM versions. Recovery is not possible — operator must re-enter.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference
member.summary:
Lightweight reference to a named secret stored in the DPAPI vault. This is the ONLY credential representation persisted in deployment checkpoints.
NEVER contains: • Plaintext passwords • Encrypted blobs • Encoded credentials • Certificate material

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.Alias
member.summary: Stable vault alias for deterministic recovery. Format: "{category}.{name}" — e.g. "domain.admin.password".

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.DeploymentId
member.summary: The deployment configuration ID this secret is bound to.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.Kind
member.summary: Semantic kind of the credential.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.Description
member.summary: Human-readable description. NEVER contains the secret value.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.CreatedAt
member.summary: UTC time this reference was created (when the secret was first vaulted).

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.ToSentinel(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the sentinel string that replaces the plaintext value in a checkpoint. Format: "__WEDM_VAULT_REF:{alias}__"

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.IsSentinel(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when the value is a vault reference sentinel.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.ExtractAlias(System.String)
member.summary: Extracts the alias from a sentinel string. Returns null if is not a valid sentinel.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.IsLegacyPlaceholder(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when the value is a legacy redacted placeholder (written by older WEDM that did not support vault references). Such values cannot be recovered from the vault.

member.name: M:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretReference.NeedsResolution(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when the field contains a value that requires rehydration (sentinel) or that indicates recovery may be impossible (legacy placeholder).

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretVaultBinding
member.summary: Binding between a and its runtime resolution state. Produced by .

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretVaultBinding.Source
member.summary: Where the secret value was sourced during rehydration.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretVaultBinding.IsResolved
member.summary: True when the secret was successfully resolved and injected into config.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretVaultBinding.ValidationError
member.summary: Validation error message when is false. NEVER contains the secret value, encrypted blob, or any credential material.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult
member.summary:
Result of a secret rehydration pass over a loaded .
If is false, resume must be blocked. The operator should be shown to understand what manual action is required.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult.AllResolved
member.summary: True only when every required secret field was resolved successfully. A false value means resume MUST be blocked.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult.Bindings
member.summary: All secret bindings examined during this rehydration pass.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult.MissingSecrets
member.summary: Aliases of secrets whose vault entries could not be found or decrypted.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult.PlaceholderSecrets
member.summary: Config field names containing legacy redacted placeholders that cannot be recovered from the vault and require operator re-entry.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult.DpapiScopeWarning
member.summary: Warning message when the current OS user differs from the vault creator's user. DPAPI CurrentUser scope binds secrets to the encrypting account — decryption under a different user account will fail.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationResult.RemediationSteps
member.summary: Human-readable remediation steps for the operator. Populated when is false.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics
member.summary: Metadata-only diagnostics for a DPAPI vault file. Contains NO secret values, encrypted blobs, or credential material.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.VaultFileExists
member.summary: True when the vault file exists on disk.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.EntryCount
member.summary: Number of entries stored in the vault.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.EntryAliases
member.summary: List of stored secret aliases (not values). Example: ["domain.admin.password", "database.sys.password"]

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.VaultOwnerUser
member.summary: OS user account that originally created the vault file.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.VaultOwnerMachine
member.summary: Machine name where the vault file was created.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.CurrentUser
member.summary: The current OS user attempting vault access.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.CurrentMachine
member.summary: The current machine name.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.ScopeCompatible
member.summary: True when DPAPI scope is likely compatible with the current execution context. False indicates a cross-user or cross-machine resume scenario that will fail decryption.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.ScopeWarning
member.summary: Warning message when DPAPI scope may be incompatible.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.VaultCreatedAt
member.summary: UTC time the vault file was first created.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.VaultDiagnostics.LastModifiedAt
member.summary: UTC time the vault file was last written.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics
member.summary: Structured diagnostics for a deployment's overall secret rehydration state. Used by and operator tooling to surface recovery status without exposing credential values.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.TotalSecretFields
member.summary: Total number of secret-holding configuration fields examined.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.VaultBoundFields
member.summary: Fields that contain vault reference sentinels (safe checkpoint state).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.PlainTextFields
member.summary: Fields that contain runtime plaintext (not yet vaulted).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.PlaceholderFields
member.summary: Fields that contain legacy "***REDACTED***" placeholders (unrecoverable).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.EmptyFields
member.summary: Fields that are empty / null (may be optional).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.ResumeReady
member.summary: True when all required secrets are vault-bound and resolvable.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.Vault
member.summary: DPAPI vault metadata for this deployment. Null when no vault file exists.

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.Issues
member.summary: Human-readable issues detected (no secret values, only field names and error types).

member.name: P:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretRehydrationDiagnostics.RemediationSteps
member.summary: Ordered remediation steps for the operator when is false.

member.name: T:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretsStorageMode
member.summary: Where operational secrets are persisted beyond in-process configuration.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretsStorageMode.ConfigOnlyRedactedLogging
member.summary: Secrets stay in configuration; logging/reporting must redact them.

member.name: F:WEDM.Domain.Models.SecretsStorageMode.DpapiLocalVault
member.summary: DPAPI-protected blobs under the local WEDM data directory (per machine user).

member.summary: Reserved — Windows Credential Manager integration (enterprise follow-up).

member.summary: When true, admin and DB passwords are also written to the DPAPI vault after validation (configuration retains copies for this session).

member.summary: When true and identity path is empty in DEV, deployment may generate a self-signed PFX under reports (non-prod only).

member.summary: Factory — create an empty result associated with a deployment.
